message:
wip(core/tests): diagnostic eprintlns in lazy_dirty_insert_after_delete path

In-progress diagnostic instrumentation inside
mod pager_routing_tests, specifically the
lazy_dirty_insert_after_delete test case.  These are temporary
DEBUG-prefixed eprintln! calls that dump pager routing state
(memdb_rows_loaded, memdb_storage_count_shortcuts_safe,
memdb_requires_active_txn_reload, pending_direct_upserts) and
the results of raw SELECT/COUNT queries both before and after the
explicit refresh that the test asserts against.  They exist so
someone is currently diagnosing why the pager-routing assertion
path behaves unexpectedly after a dirty-read-boundary INSERT and
need concrete per-step state in the test output.

This commit preserves the diagnostics exactly as they were found
in the working tree so no investigation state is lost.  The
eprintln output is invisible to successful runs (stderr is
only surfaced on failure or with `cargo test -- --nocapture`), so
it does not change test correctness, just verbosity.

Follow-up: once the root cause of the pager routing anomaly is
understood these eprintlns should be either deleted or replaced
with a focused assertion/helper that locks in the invariant
being probed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
